original_hypothesis.title: Irrecoverable lockout for pre-existing landlords due to missing/rarely-callable plot metadata initialization path (triggerPlotMetadata)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: updateTaxRate requires plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated != 0. For landlords with pre-existing locks prior to LandManager deployment, the intended path is triggerPlotMetadata() called by the user. If landlords fail to call it and AccountManager doesn't proactively call updatePlotMetadata(), they may be unable to set tax rates (and potentially have inconsistent farming/tax snapshot behavior for renters).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational dead-end state risk: real systems often have accounts that never complete a manual migration step. While not a direct exploit by an attacker, it creates predictable misbehavior and may be used for griefing (e.g., encouraging staking to landlords whose tax settings cannot be updated) or causes governance/support incidents.
deployment_assumptions: Some landlords existed with locked value before LandManager deployment
AccountManager does not guarantee calling updatePlotMetadata(landlord) for every landlord before they need updateTaxRate
Landlords may not call triggerPlotMetadata() themselves
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Project docs explicitly mention triggerPlotMetadata is 'Only to be triggered ... if they had locked before the land manager was deployed' (LandManager.sol comment). No deployment scripts shown that batch-initialize plot metadata for all landlords.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateTaxRate: if (plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated == 0) revert PlotMetadataNotUpdatedError(); triggerPlotMetadata() is the manual initialization path

preconditions: Landlord attempts updateTaxRate() (or renters depend on the landlord’s tax rate changing from DEFAULT)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Manual one-time initialization requirement suggests a migration step that can be missed in production.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Depends on AccountManager operational behavior; may require a batch job or user action to avoid lockout.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Persisting until triggerPlotMetadata or updatePlotMetadata is called for that landlord.

operational_questions: Does AccountManager call updatePlotMetadata for every landlord upon registration/lock changes?
What percentage of active landlords have plotMetadata.lastUpdated == 0 on-chain?
What happens to stakers who stake to landlords with uninitialized metadata (tax snapshots)?
validation_requirements: On-chain scan: count landlords with locked value but plotMetadata.lastUpdated==0
Review AccountManager logic and workflows for updatePlotMetadata calls
Test staking/farming to a landlord with lastUpdated==0 and determine tax behavior

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify landlords with many plots but plotMetadata not initialized.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Stake to those landlords; exploit predictability of DEFAULT_TAX_RATE snapshot vs inability of landlord to change tax.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Use resulting economic mispricing (if any) until landlord fixes metadata.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Landlords may be unable to manage tax settings; can distort incentives and cause user disputes
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low to medium (requires correct operational procedure, but may be hard to coordinate at scale)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Tax management and potentially tax snapshots during stake

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Most likely during initial migration and early lifecycle

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: plotMetadata[lastUpdated]==0 for landlords with nonzero lockManager value
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Stakers concentrating on landlords with stuck metadata

validation_result.mechanism: manual migration step - not a code defect
validation_result.impact_summary: The described scenario is an operational concern, not a code defect. The code explicitly provides triggerPlotMetadata() as a self-service recovery path, making any lockout trivially recoverable by the affected landlord.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis claims an 'irrecoverable lockout' for pre-existing landlords who haven't initialized their plot metadata. However, the code deliberately provides triggerPlotMetadata() as a manual initialization path, and this is documented in the code comments. The landlord is never permanently locked out—they can call triggerPlotMetadata() at any time to initialize their metadata and subsequently use updateTaxRate(). Furthermore, even while metadata is uninitialized, the landlord's plots remain functional: stakers can still stake via stakeMunchable() (which does not check lastUpdated), and farming proceeds with a 0% tax rate (since currentTaxRate defaults to 0 for uninitialized struct). The landlord simply misses out on tax revenue until they call triggerPlotMetadata(). This is a deliberate migration design, not a code defect.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: LandManager.sol provides three distinct paths for metadata initialization: (1) triggerPlotMetadata() for self-service by pre-existing landlords, (2) updatePlotMetadata() called by AccountManager during lock operations, and (3) initialization via stakeMunchable's implicit reliance on default struct values. The updateTaxRate check at plotMetadata[landlord].lastUpdated == 0 is a deliberate guard that prevents tax changes before initialization, not a bug. The recovery path (triggerPlotMetadata) is always available to the affected landlord.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: This is the guard that blocks uninitialized landlords from updating tax rates. However, it is a deliberate design choice, not a defect. The landlord can resolve this by calling triggerPlotMetadata().
This function exists precisely to handle the pre-existing landlord scenario. It is the designed recovery path. Any landlord can call this to initialize their metadata.

calling_sequence: 5. plotMetadata[mainAccount] is initialized with lastUpdated = block.timestamp, currentTaxRate = DEFAULT_TAX_RATE
6. Landlord can now successfully call updateTaxRate()
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The code path is functioning exactly as designed. The revert on uninitialized metadata is intentional, and the triggerPlotMetadata() function provides the recovery mechanism. There is no irrecoverable state.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Some landlords existed with locked value before LandManager deployment
AccountManager does not guarantee calling updatePlotMetadata(landlord) for every landlord before they need updateTaxRate
Landlords may not call triggerPlotMetadata() themselves
config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that batch-initialize plot metadata for all landlords. The upgrade-proxy-contract.ts script handles LandManager proxy upgrades but does not include any migration logic for pre-existing landlords. However, the absence of batch initialization does not create a code defect—the code explicitly provides triggerPlotMetadata() as a self-service solution.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The preliminary attack methodology describes staking to landlords with uninitialized metadata to exploit a 'predictable DEFAULT_TAX_RATE snapshot.' However, when metadata is uninitialized, currentTaxRate defaults to 0 (Solidity struct default), meaning stakers would pay 0% tax—this actually benefits stakers, not the attacker. The landlord simply misses tax revenue until they call triggerPlotMetadata(). There is no economic exploit or DoS beyond the landlord's own inaction.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is purely operational and self-inflicted. A landlord who fails to call triggerPlotMetadata() cannot adjust their tax rate, resulting in a 0% default tax rate applied to stakers on their plots. The landlord loses potential tax revenue but can resolve this at any time. Stakers are not harmed—they benefit from the lower tax rate. NFTs are never locked, and farming proceeds normally.
recommended_mitigations: Consider adding a UI notification or off-chain monitoring for landlords with uninitialized metadata
Optionally, allow updateTaxRate to auto-initialize metadata if lastUpdated == 0 (combining triggerPlotMetadata logic), though this changes the deliberate design
Add documentation/onboarding flows to prompt pre-existing landlords to call triggerPlotMetadata()
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LandManager behind proxy. 2. Have a landlord lock value in LockManager (simulating pre-deployment lock). 3. Verify landlord cannot call updateTaxRate (reverts with PlotMetadataNotUpdatedError). 4. Have landlord call triggerPlotMetadata(). 5. Verify landlord can now call updateTaxRate successfully. 6. Confirm the 'lockout' is always recoverable.
uncertainties: AccountManager source code is not provided, so we cannot verify when/if it calls updatePlotMetadata for landlords during lock operations
The actual DEFAULT_TAX_RATE value depends on ConfigStorage configuration which may have its own issues (separate from this hypothesis)
additional_testing_needed: Verify AccountManager's lock/unlock flows to confirm whether updatePlotMetadata is called during normal lock operations
Test staking to a landlord with uninitialized metadata to confirm currentTaxRate defaults to 0
